source.candidateWorkingTree: modified only on the recorded CI, runner, test, changelog, manifest, and receipt paths

surface: developer and pull-request deterministic test feedback

workload.purpose: Run the complete Gauntlet workflow regression suite without reducing cases, assertions, retries, or failure behavior.
workload.setup: One warm-up preceded three measured samples. Five runner lanes were available to both implementations.
workload.correctnessOracle: All 11 named top-level workflow cases exit zero.

profile.diagnosis: The single GitHub Actions job called every independent top-level case sequentially.
profile.hypothesis: Scheduling five isolated domain groups concurrently should reduce wall time toward the slower of the PRD and orchestration groups without changing the correctness oracle.
profile.prediction: Approximately 28 to 35 seconds locally, plus GitHub runner setup in hosted CI.
profile.falsification: Reject the change if any case is skipped, group execution is order-dependent, the aggregate gate can pass after a child failure, or candidate timing overlaps the baseline range.

change.summary: Add five workflow groups, run them as a GitHub Actions matrix, and retain one stable aggregate gauntlet job.
change.complexity: Five hosted runners are used concurrently instead of one; no dependency or cache was added.
change.displacedCosts: Hosted runner-minutes remain approximately similar and checkout/setup work is repeated five times.

correctness.groupCoverage: All 11 cases appear exactly once across policy, install, lifecycle, prd, and orchestration.
correctness.aggregateGate: The gauntlet job runs with always() and fails unless the matrix result is success.

adjacentMetrics.localCpuContention: Candidate samples ran five processes on one machine and still improved materially.
adjacentMetrics.githubHostedWallTime: Unmeasured until the workflow runs on GitHub.
adjacentMetrics.runnerConsumption: Expected to remain similar or increase slightly from repeated setup.

limitations: Local parallel timing does not include GitHub queue and runner startup.
Branch protection is currently disabled, so the aggregate job is preserved for future re-enablement but is not presently enforced by GitHub.
invalidatedBy: Changing the 11-case workload or its assertions.
Changing Python, operating system, machine, cache policy, or available runner lanes.
Changing the workflow grouping or benchmark harness.
